revision.text: #redirect[[en:Osugi_Shrine,_Saitama]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''大杉神社'''（おおすぎじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[上福岡市]]中福岡にある小さな[[神社]]である。明治11年に建てられた。本社は[[茨城県]][[桜川村]]にある大杉神社で、祭神は大物主大神である。
[[画像:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|thumb|神社と新河岸川]]
===歴史=== [[新河岸川]]の舟運に従事した人々が、船の安全を願って明治11年 ([[1878年]]) に建立した。以後、川を上り下りする船頭の信仰を集めた。船頭は毎年二人を選んで本社に送り、船中安全のお札をもらう慣わしであった。 ===立地と建造物=== 神社は新河岸川の西岸に位置し、道ではなく川を向いている。鳥居も川面に向いてうつむくように少し傾いている。かつては新河岸川を通行する人が、船上から神社に賽銭を投げて安全を祈ったという。鳥居には「大杉宮」と記された額がある。 神社が祀られる前から、この場所には船中安全・村中安穏を祈願する人がいた。小さな境内の裏側には、文政9年 ([[1826年]]) に建てられた石碑があり、大杉宮、船玉宮、水天宮の三社大明神を鎮座たてまつる旨が記されている。 ===祭礼=== 祭礼は[[9月27日]]。大正時代までは船神楽、芝居が行なわれて賑わった。

神社と新河岸川}} '''大杉神社'''（おおすぎじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[ふじみ野市]]中福岡にある小さな[[神社]]である。[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）に建てられた。本社は[[茨城県]][[稲敷市]]にある[[大杉神社]]で、祭神は[[大物主|大物主大神]]である。 ==歴史== [[新河岸川]]の舟運に従事した人々が、船の安全を願って明治11年 （[[1878年]]） に建立した。以後、川を上り下りする船頭の信仰を集めた。船頭は毎年二人を選んで本社に送り、船中安全のお札をもらう慣わしであった。 ==立地と建造物== 神社は新河岸川の西岸に位置し、道ではなく川を向いている。鳥居も川面に向いてうつむくように少し傾いている。かつては新河岸川を通行する人が、船上から神社に賽銭を投げて安全を祈ったという。鳥居には「大杉宮」と記された額がある。 神社が祀られる前から、この場所には船中安全・村中安穏を祈願する人がいた。小さな境内の裏側には、文政9年 （[[1826年]]） に建てられた石碑があり、大杉宮、船玉宮、水天宮の三社大明神を鎮座たてまつる旨が記されている。 ==祭礼== 祭礼は[[9月27日]]。大正時代までは船神楽、芝居が行なわれて賑わった。 {{DEFAULTSORT:おおすきしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:ふじみ野市]]

#redirect[[en:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Removed redirect to [[en:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Ōsugi Shrine | Location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} | Deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主}} | Founded = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | Image = [[Image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11年}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == Location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[ja:大杉神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Ōsugi Shrine | Location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} | Deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主}} | Founded = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | Image = [[Image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11年}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == Location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]]

The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. revision.comment: mass editing two
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主}} | consecrated = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | image = [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11年}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]]

It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11年}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主}} | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11年}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主}} | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11th year }} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9th year }} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|12=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|12=draft}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11th year|12=draft}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|12=draft}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|12=draft}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|12=draft}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9th year|12=draft}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|12=draft}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日|12=draft}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[en:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osugi Shrine, Saitama}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|12=draft}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|12=draft}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11th year|12=draft}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|12=draft}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|12=draft}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|12=draft}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9th year|12=draft}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|12=draft}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日|12=draft}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[en:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osugi Shrine, Saitama}} [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]]

The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|12=draft|12=draft}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|12=draft|12=draft}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[en:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osugi Shrine, Saitama}}

revision.comment: 19 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市) up to rev 79726531
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=大杉神社 | 所在地=埼玉県ふじみ野市福岡新田 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|52|39.7|N|139|32|29.9|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神=大物主大神 | 創建=明治11年 （1878年） | 社格= | 例祭=9月27日 | 本殿= | 画像=[[画像:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
神社と新河岸川 }} '''大杉神社'''（おおすぎじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[ふじみ野市]]中福岡にある小さな[[神社]]である。[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）に建てられた。本社は[[茨城県]][[稲敷市]]にある[[大杉神社]]で、祭神は[[大物主|大物主大神]]である。 ==歴史== [[新河岸川]]の舟運に従事した人々が、船の安全を願って明治11年 （[[1878年]]） に建立した。以後、川を上り下りする船頭の信仰を集めた。船頭は毎年二人を選んで本社に送り、船中安全のお札をもらう慣わしであった。 ==立地と建造物== 神社は新河岸川の西岸に位置し、道ではなく川を向いている。鳥居も川面に向いてうつむくように少し傾いている。かつては新河岸川を通行する人が、船上から神社に賽銭を投げて安全を祈ったという。鳥居には「大杉宮」と記された額がある。 神社が祀られる前から、この場所には船中安全・村中安穏を祈願する人がいた。小さな境内の裏側には、文政9年 （[[1826年]]） に建てられた石碑があり、大杉宮、船玉宮、水天宮の三社大明神を鎮座たてまつる旨が記されている。 ==祭礼== 祭礼は[[9月27日]]。大正時代までは船神楽、芝居が行なわれて賑わった。 {{DEFAULTSORT:おおすきしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:ふじみ野市の歴史]] [[Category:ふじみ野市の建築物]]

神社と新河岸川 }} '''大杉神社'''（おおすぎじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[ふじみ野市]]中福岡にある小さな[[神社]]である。[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）に建てられた。本社は[[茨城県]][[稲敷市]]にある[[大杉神社]]で、祭神は[[大物主|大物主大神]]である。 ==歴史== [[新河岸川]]の舟運に従事した人々が、船の安全を願って明治11年 （[[1878年]]） に建立した。以後、川を上り下りする船頭の信仰を集めた。船頭は毎年二人を選んで本社に送り、船中安全のお札をもらう慣わしであった。 ==立地と建造物== 神社は新河岸川の西岸に位置し、道ではなく川を向いている。鳥居も川面に向いてうつむくように少し傾いている。かつては新河岸川を通行する人が、船上から神社に賽銭を投げて安全を祈ったという。鳥居には「大杉宮」と記された額がある。 神社が祀られる前から、この場所には船中安全・村中安穏を祈願する人がいた。小さな境内の裏側には、文政9年 （[[1826年]]） に建てられた石碑があり、大杉宮、船玉宮、水天宮の三社大明神を鎮座たてまつる旨が記されている。 ==祭礼== 祭礼は[[9月27日]]。大正時代までは船神楽、芝居が行なわれて賑わった。 {{DEFAULTSORT:おおすきしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:ふじみ野市の歴史]] [[Category:ふじみ野市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主}} | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11th year}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9th year}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[en:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osugi Shrine, Saitama}} {{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 11|qq=}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingashi River|qq=}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|qq=}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Suitengū Shrine|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū Shrine|qq=}} were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 27|qq=}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[en:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|qq=}} | deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi Ōkami]] | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 11|qq=}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]] to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|qq=}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|qq=}}, and [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on [[:en:September 27|September 27]]. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujimino, Saitama]] [[Category:ふじみ野市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]]

Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}. It was built in {{ill|Meiji 11|ja|明治11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 11|qq=}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]] to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|qq=}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|qq=}}, and [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on [[:en:September 27|September 27]]. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujimino City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujimino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujimino, Saitama]] [[Category:ふじみ野市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi Ōkami]] | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was built in [[:en:1878|Meiji 11]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]] to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on [[:en:September 27|September 27]]. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujimino, Saitama]] [[Category:ふじみ野市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11435951]] Wikidata name: Ōsugi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ill|35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/35°52′39.7″N 139°32′29.9″E|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi Ōkami]] | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Shrine and Shingashi River }} '''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was built in [[:en:1878|Meiji 11]] (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}. == History == Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]] to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels. == location and Structures == The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮). Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] were enshrined there. == Festival == The shrine's main festival is held annually on [[:en:September 27|September 27]]. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays. {{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Osugi Shrine, Saitama]] [[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujimino, Saitama]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fujimino]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11435951]] Wikidata name: Ōsugi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōsugi Shrine | location = Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.877694|N|139.541639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701}} | consecration_year = Meiji 11 (1878) | Main Festival = September 27 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:OsugiJinja2004.jpg | prefecture = {{ill|Fujimino|qid=Q873590}} }}
'''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was built in [[:en:1878|Meiji 11]] (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}.
== History ==
Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]] to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels.
== location and Structures ==
The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮).
Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] were enshrined there.
== Festival ==
The shrine's main festival is held annually on [[:en:September 27|September 27]]. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays.

'''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was built in [[:en:1878|Meiji 11]] (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}.
== History ==
Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]] to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels.
== location and Structures ==
The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮).
Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] were enshrined there.
== Festival ==
The shrine's main festival is held annually on [[:en:September 27|September 27]]. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays.
{{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was built in [[:en:1878|Meiji 11]] (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}.
== History ==
Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]] to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels.
== location and Structures ==
The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮).
Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengū Shrine]] were enshrined there.
== Festival ==
The shrine's main festival is held annually on [[:en:September 27|September 27]]. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays.
{{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
[[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]]

'''Ōsugi Shrine''' (大杉神社) is a small {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県ふじみ野市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimino City, Saitama Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was built in {{ill|1878|en|1878|WD=Q7776|lt=Meiji 11}} (1878). The main shrine is located in {{ill|Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県稲敷市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inashiki City, Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|ja|大物主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}.
== History ==
Ōsugi Shrine was established in 1878 by people working in river transport on the {{ill|Shingashi River|en|Shingashi River|WD=Q11502669|lt=Shingashi River}} to pray for safe voyages. Since then, it has gathered faith from boatmen traveling the river. It became customary to select two boatmen annually to visit the main shrine to receive a talisman ensuring the safety of their vessels.
== location and Structures ==
The shrine is located on the western bank of the Shingashi River and faces the river instead of the road. The torii gate leans slightly forward, as if bowing toward the river. It is said that travelers on the Shingashi River once threw coins from their boats to pray for safety. The torii gate bears a plaque inscribed with "Ōsugi-gū" (大杉宮).
Even before the shrine was established, the site was used for prayers for the safety of boats and the peace of the village. Behind the small shrine grounds is a stone monument erected in {{ill|Bunsei 9|ja|文政9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1826), which states that the deities of Ōsugi-gū, {{ill|Funadama Shrine|ja|船玉宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suitengū Shrine}} were enshrined there.
== Festival ==
The shrine's main festival is held annually on {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|WD=Q2884|lt=September 27}}. During the Taishō era, it was a lively event featuring boat performances and plays.
{{translated page|ja|大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)|version=79726531|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[image:OsugiJinja2004.jpg|250px]]
[[ja:大杉神社 (ふじみ野市)]] [[ja:大杉神社]]
